zh.lifeSummary: 當Joanna Ross於1345年出生在英國蘇格蘭吉德布萊次爾時，她的父親William - Uilleam de Ross 5th Earl of Ross, Lord of Skye36歲，母親Mary MacDonald27歲。她於1375年與Alexander Fraser of Cowie and Durris - 1st of Philorth在蘇格蘭可洛馬迪夏爾可洛馬迪結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1400年在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾考維享年55歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joanna Ross于1345年出生在英国苏格兰吉德布萊次爾时，她的父亲William - Uilleam de Ross 5th Earl of Ross, Lord of Skye36岁，母亲Mary MacDonald27岁。她于1375年与Alexander Fraser of Cowie and Durris - 1st of Philorth在苏格兰可洛馬迪夏爾可洛馬迪结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1400年在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾考維享年55岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Ross
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

variants.name: Ruskin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Tauba, Uzi.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rosce (see Ross 4) + the diminutive suffix -kin.
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish (Ashkenazic) surname, for example   Raskin  .

variants.name: Ros
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.
Swedish: ornamental name from ros ‘rose’.

variants.name: Rose
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rossell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rusling
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Rocelin (Old French Roscelin, Rocelin, ancient Germanic Ruozelin), a double diminutive of Rozo (see Ross ).

variants.name: Rohs
meanings: German: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Joris.
German: topographic name from Middle High German rœsse ‘kiln’, in particular one used for drying hemp and flax or for burning lime.
English and Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Ross  .

nameMeanings.name: Joanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joanna Ross was born in 1345, in Kirkcudbrightshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, William - Uilleam de Ross 5th Earl of Ross, Lord of Skye, was 36 and her mother, Mary MacDonald, was 27. She married Alexander Fraser of Cowie and Durris - 1st of Philorth in 1375, in Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1400, in Cowie, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, at the age of 55, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joanna Ross est née en 1345, à Kirkcudbrightshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, William - Uilleam de Ross 5th Earl of Ross, Lord of Skye, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Mary MacDonald, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Alexander Fraser of Cowie and Durris - 1st of Philorth en 1375, à Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1400, à Cowie, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joanna Ross 1345 in Kirkcudbrightshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William - Uilleam de Ross 5th Earl of Ross, Lord of Skye 36 und ihre Mutter Mary MacDonald 27. Sie hat 1375 in Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland Alexander Fraser of Cowie and Durris - 1st of Philorth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1400 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Cowie, Aberdeenshire, Scotland und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joanna Ross è nata il nel 1345 nel Kirkcudbrightshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, William - Uilleam de Ross 5th Earl of Ross, Lord of Skye, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Mary MacDonald, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Alexander Fraser of Cowie and Durris - 1st of Philorth nel 1375, nel Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1400, nel Cowie, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolta a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Joanna Rossが1345年にイギリススコットランドキルククウドブリトシュアイアで生まれた時，父William - Uilleam de Ross 5th Earl of Ross, Lord of Skyeは36歳，母Mary MacDonaldは27歳であった。1375年にスコットランドクロマルトイシュアイアクロマルトイでAlexander Fraser of Cowie and Durris - 1st of Philorthと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1400年にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアコウィで55歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joanna Ross(이)가 1345년 영국 스코틀랜드 커kc드브라이츠히르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William - Uilleam de Ross 5th Earl of Ross, Lord of Skye(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary MacDonald(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1375년 스컷랜드 크로마티실르 크로마티에서 Alexander Fraser of Cowie and Durris - 1st of Philorth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1400년 스컷랜드 압르디실르 코위에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joanna Ross nasceu em 1345, em Kirkcudbrightshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, William - Uilleam de Ross 5th Earl of Ross, Lord of Skye, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Mary MacDonald, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Alexander Fraser of Cowie and Durris - 1st of Philorth em 1375, em Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1400, em Cowie, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, com 55 anos, e foi sepultada em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joanna Ross родилась в 1345 г. в местности кирккудбраицхир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William - Uilleam de Ross 5th Earl of Ross, Lord of Skye было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary MacDonald, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alexander Fraser of Cowie and Durris - 1st of Philorth в 1375 г. в местности кромерти, кромертишир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1400 г. в местности коуи, абирдиншир, Шотландия в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joanna Ross nació en 1345, en Kirkcudbrightshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, William - Uilleam de Ross 5th Earl of Ross, Lord of Skye, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Mary MacDonald, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Alexander Fraser of Cowie and Durris - 1st of Philorth en 1375, en Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1400, en Cowie, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultada en Scotland.
